data.title: "Crypto Market Turmoil: Bitcoin's Retreat, Memecoin Frenzy, and Bullish Signs Emerge"
data.description:
Despite recent market volatility, a glass-half-full perspective reveals several positive indicators and developments in the crypto and broader financial landscape.

Bitcoin, the world's largest cryptocurrency, has retreated from its all-time high reached last month, with US bitcoin ETFs experiencing record outflows. However, this downturn is not isolated to the crypto market, as stocks have also seen red. Nevertheless, a rebound could be on the horizon, as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite indexes remained relatively stable despite Bitcoin's decline.

21Shares crypto research strategist Matt Mena suggests that bull markets peak under specific conditions, such as excessive leverage, retail euphoria, and falling Bitcoin dominance, rather than compressed funding rates. He argues that liquidations reset overleveraged positions, creating healthier uptrends as traders re-enter the market post-shakeout.

The memecoin token frenzy, exemplified by tokens like $TRUMP, aligns with mid-cycle speculation patterns observed in 2020-2021. Blockworks' Katherine Ross notes that recent token launches have contributed to the larger exhaustion in the market. However, Bitwise research head ryan Rasmussen counters this perspective, highlighting the potential for a market recovery.

Mena also points out that the M2 money supply has been growing since mid-January, and quantitative tightening may soon reach a natural end. Additionally, the Pi Cycle Top Indicator, which tracks Bitcoin's 111-day simple moving average against a 2x multiple of the 350-day SMA, has not yet signaled a market peak.

LMAX Group's Joel Kruger argues that correlations between Bitcoin and traditional risk assets can be misleading, as Bitcoin can generate significant demand as an attractive portfolio diversification asset. He believes there is formidable support for Bitcoin in the $70,000-$75,000 zone, which should serve as an attractive higher low ahead of the next major upside extension and bullish continuation to a fresh record high beyond $110,000.

Last week's Bybit hack is unlikely to have a major impact on institutional confidence in centralized exchanges, according to YouHodler markets chief Ruslan Lienkha. He believes that institutions typically adhere to strict treasury management rules and allocate only the necessary liquidity to CEXs for routine transactions, while large-scale institutional trades are

data.title: Bonk vs. Dogecoin vs. Pepe: Crypto Meme Coin Showdown in 2025
data.description:

PEPE, meanwhile, sits at $0.00002052, down but not out.

Dogecoin, the original crypto meme, has maintained value above $0.2 with a $30 billion market capitalization. However, it has experienced a 12% price fall in 24 hours, with a circulating supply of 148 billion coins and a daily trading volume of $3.55 billion. Dogecoin's unlimited supply and meme roots make it a wild card in the uncertain 2025 market.

It's down 9% in the last day but has seen massive trading activity with a daily volume of $205 million. data.title: "Safe Admits Developer Machine Compromise in $1.4B Bybit Hack, CZ Demands Clarity"
data.description:
Safe, the developer of the SafeWallet multisignature product used by Bybit, has released a post-mortem update following the recent hack that resulted in a $1.4 billion loss. The update revealed that the root cause of the incident was a compromised developer machine. However, this announcement has drawn criticism from Binance co-founder Changpeng "CZ" Zhao.

According to Safe, the forensic review did not find any vulnerabilities in the Safe smart contracts or the code of its front-end portal and services. Instead, the compromised machine was modified to target the Bybit Safe and divert transactions to a different hardware wallet. Martin Köppelmann, the co-founder of the Gnosis blockchain network, which developed Safe, noted that the hackers managed to trick multiple signers into approving the malicious transaction.

CZ criticized the update, stating that it used vague language to brush over the issues. He asked for clarification on several points, including how the hackers accessed Bybit's systems, how they tricked multiple signers, and why they did not target other addresses. Köppelmann speculated that the threat actors did not target other addresses to prevent discovery and detection.

A forensic review conducted by Sygnia and Verichains revealed that the credentials of a Safe developer were compromised, allowing the attacker to gain unauthorized access to the Safe infrastructure and deceive signers into approving a malicious transaction.

Meanwhile, onchain data shows that the Lazarus Group has transferred 45,900 Ether (ETH), valued at approximately $113 million, in the last 24 hours. This brings the total amount of funds laundered to over 135,000 ETH, valued at roughly $335 million. Analyst EmberCN estimates that the Lazarus Group will likely have "cleaned up" the funds within 8-10 days. Bybit and blockchain analytics firm Elliptic have tracked the stolen crypto to over 11,000 wallets controlled by the Lazarus Group, and Elliptic has published a data feed of addresses associated with the hacker group to help market participants stay clear of those wallet addresses and prevent money laundering.

Hybrid models bridge gaps between retail and institutions—watch this space." This sentiment aligns with whale activity shifting away from DOGE and PEPE, and towards infrastructure projects. As the best new crypto to invest in, the DTX Exchange presale price of $0.18 offers a strategic entry before listings, with early adopters eyeing the $0.36 launch target.

The Dogecoin price has faced sustained pressure, dropping 6% in 24 hours during declining whale transactions. Large DOGE holders reduced activity by 56% since November 2024, contributing to weakened liquidity and a 34% monthly decline. Evaluators attribute this to profit-taking and portfolio diversification into emerging utility tokens, including the new DTX Exchange project attracting institutional attention.

data.title: Trump's Venezuela Oil License Revocation: A Blow to Maduro, but What About Chevron?
data.description:
President Donald Trump's decision to revoke the "concessions" granted to Venezuela by the Biden administration, including the license for chevron to operate in the country, has significant implications for the global oil market and Chevron's financial performance. The move, announced on February 27, 2025, aims to put pressure on the Nicolás Maduro regime by cutting off a crucial source of revenue. However, it also raises questions about the future of Chevron's operations in Venezuela and the potential impact on U.S. energy security.

Revocation of Chevron's License: A Blow to Maduro

The revocation of Chevron's license is a significant setback for the Maduro regime, which has been struggling to maintain its grip on power amidst widespread protests and economic turmoil. Chevron's operations in Venezuela have been a vital source of revenue for the government, accounting for about 20% of the country's oil production. With the license revoked, Chevron will likely cease its operations, leading to a significant reduction in Venezuelan oil production and exports.

Impact on Global Oil Supply and Demand

The revocation of Chevron's license could have significant implications for global oil supply and demand dynamics. Venezuela holds the world's largest proven oil reserves, estimated to be around 300 billion barrels. By reducing access to these significant oil reserves, the U.S. is effectively decreasing global oil supply, which could put upward pressure on oil prices. This could have knock-on effects on global economic growth, as higher oil prices can lead to increased inflation and reduced consumer spending.

Moreover, the revocation of Chevron's license could exacerbate geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, as Iran may see it as an opportunity to increase its influence in the region. This could lead to further instability and potential disruptions in oil supply from the Middle East, further impacting global oil prices.

Potential Impact on U.S. Energy Security

The decision to revoke Chevron's license could have implications for U.S. energy security, as Venezuela is a significant source of oil for the U.S. market. With less oil coming from Venezuela, the U.S. may need to rely more heavily on other sources, potentially increasing its dependence on countries like Saudi Arabia or Russia. This could expose the U.S. to geopolitical risks associated with these countries.

Chevron's Financial Performance and Future Growth Prospects

The revocation of Chevron's license could have significant implications for the company's financial performance and future growth prospects. Chevron's operations in Venezuela have been a significant source of revenue, and the disruption of these profitable activities could lead to a decrease in revenue and profits. Additionally, the company's dependence on Venezuelan heavy crude makes it vulnerable to changes in U.S. policy towards Venezuela, exposing it to geopolitical risks.

Investors should consider the following strategic adjustments in light of these developments:

1. Diversify Portfolio: Investors may want to diversify their energy holdings to reduce exposure to geopolitical risks. This could involve investing in companies with operations in more stable regions or in other sectors of the energy industry.
2. Monitor Chevron's Response: Investors should closely monitor Chevron's response to the license revocation. The company may need to make strategic adjustments to its operations, such as reducing its exposure to Venezuela or increasing investment in other regions.
3. Assess the Impact on U.S. Oil Supply: The potential disruption to U.S. oil supply could have broader implications for the U.S. economy. Investors should assess the potential impact on U.S. oil prices and the broader economy.
4. Stay Informed: Investors should stay informed about developments in U.S. policy towards Venezuela and the broader geopolitical situation. This could involve monitoring news reports, policy announcements, and other relevant information.

In conclusion, President Trump's decision to revoke Chevron's license to operate in Venezuela could have significant implications for global oil supply and demand dynamics, U.S. energy security, and Chevron's financial performance. Investors should consider these implications and make strategic adjustments to their portfolios accordingly.

Bitcoin ETFs Face Billion-Dollar Outflows Amid Crypto Market Turmoil
data.description:
Bitcoin ETFs are experiencing significant outflows as the market grapples with falling crypto prices amid heightened macroeconomic uncertainty. On Tuesday, spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) witnessed over $1.1 billion in outflows, marking a substantial shift in investor sentiment. According to Bloomberg ETF Research Analyst James Seyffart, "The outflow numbers seem huge when you’re talking billions, but it’s only about 2.3% of total assets."

The recent volatility in the cryptocurrency market has led to dramatic outflows from Bitcoin ETFs, which have seen more than $2 billion shed in February alone. This sharp decline follows a tumultuous series of events affecting risk assets broadly, including concerns over inflation and potential trade wars. Investors are taking a cautious approach, leading many to retreat from the volatile crypto market.

The $1.4 billion hack of the Bybit exchange last Friday further exacerbated pressure on the crypto market. This incident has contributed to a growing anxiety among investors regarding the safety of crypto assets. Factors such as the hack and general market selloff due to potential macroeconomic downturns are crucial contributors to the recent outflows from ETFs.

Despite recent challenges, the overall performance of U.S.-listed crypto ETFs remains notable. They have amassed around $3.1 billion in net inflows this year, indicating that there is still significant interest in cryptocurrencies. Market analysts, including ETF.com Analyst Sumit Roy, highlighted that despite consecutive downtrends faced by tech-focused assets, investor confidence in crypto ETFs is resilient amid the turmoil.

As Bitcoin’s price fell below $84,000—a level not seen since November—the market has reacted with cautious sentiment. Over the last week, Bitcoin has dropped approximately 12%, with other major cryptocurrencies like Ethereum and XRP also experiencing significant declines. The continuous downward trend in Bitcoin’s value could signal potential shifts in the profitability and attractiveness of crypto investments for both individual and institutional investors.

Despite the rocky situation, there is anticipation surrounding new ETF applications, with several asset managers pursuing products based on other cryptocurrencies beyond Bitcoin. These applications include ETFs linked to assets like XRP, Litecoin, and Polkadot, showcasing a continued interest in diversified digital asset portfolios. data.title: Robinhood Rallies: DraftKings Stops the Slide
Eyenovia Shares Surge After Regaining Nasdaq Compliance: What's Going On?
data.description:
Eyenovia, Inc. (NASDAQ: EYEN) shares have been on a rollercoaster ride recently, with the stock surging following the company's announcement that it has regained compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements. But what exactly is going on with eyenovia, and why should investors pay attention to this ophthalmic technology company?



Eyenovia's proprietary Optejet® device is a topical ophthalmic medication dispensing platform that enhances patient compliance and safety in medication dispensing, particularly for chronic front-of-the-eye diseases. The company's focus on this innovative technology has caught the attention of investors, as Eyenovia's stock price has been volatile in recent months.

On February 26, 2025, Eyenovia announced that it had regained compliance with all Nasdaq Capital Market continued listing requirements, including the minimum bid price and low-priced stocks rules. This news led to a surge in the company's share price, with Eyenovia shares up 26.5% at $1.91 at the time of writing (Benzinga Pro).

The company attributed this achievement to a recent reverse stock split, which helped boost its share price above the required threshold. CEO Michael Rowe emphasized that maintaining the Nasdaq listing, along with other steps taken to restructure the company and accelerate development of the user-filled Optejet device, represents an important milestone for Eyenovia as it continues to explore strategic alternatives to maximize shareholder value.



However, the reverse stock split may have raised concerns about liquidity and trading volume, as the significant reduction in the number of outstanding shares could potentially make the stock more volatile. Additionally, the need for a reverse stock split to maintain its Nasdaq listing could signal a lack of investor confidence and negatively impact the company's perception in the market.

To ensure future growth, Eyenovia must focus on executing its business strategy, driving sales growth of its approved products, and successfully developing its late-stage asset in pediatric progressive myopia, MicroPine. By doing so, the company can enhance its long-term performance and market position, ultimately creating value for shareholders.

In conclusion, Eyenovia's recent surge in share price following its regain of Nasdaq compliance is an encouraging sign for investors. However, it is essential to remain cautious and monitor the company's progress closely, as the reverse stock split and potential volatility may pose challenges to its long-term success. By keeping a close eye on Eyenovia's developments and maintaining a balanced perspective, investors can make informed decisions about their investments in this innovative ophthalmic technology company.

data.title: Crypto Markets Plummet $100B Amid Trade Tensions
data.description:
Cryptocurrency markets are grappling with the fallout from escalating trade tensions, as investors grapple with uncertainty and volatility. The global cryptocurrency market capitalization has plummeted by over $100 billion in the past week, with Bitcoin, the world's largest cryptocurrency, losing more than 10% of its value.

The trade war between the United States and China has been a significant factor in the recent market downturn. The imposition of tariffs on Chinese goods by the U.S. administration has led to a tit-for-tat response from Beijing, creating uncertainty and dampening investor sentiment. This has spilled over into the cryptocurrency market, with investors seeking safer havens for their assets.

Analysts have noted that the cryptocurrency market is particularly sensitive to geopolitical risks, as it is still in its early stages of development. The lack of regulatory clarity and the absence of a clear path to profitability have made investors more risk-averse, leading to a sell-off in cryptocurrency markets.

Bitcoin, which has traditionally been seen as a safe haven asset, has also been affected by the trade tensions. Its price has fallen below the psychologically important $10,000 mark, raising concerns about its ability to maintain its status as a store of value. However, some analysts remain optimistic about Bitcoin's long-term prospects, citing its decentralized nature and potential for use as a medium of exchange.

The impact of trade policy uncertainty on cryptocurrency markets is not limited to Bitcoin. Other major cryptocurrencies, such as Ethereum and Ripple, have also experienced significant price declines. The total market capitalization of the cryptocurrency market has fallen by more than 20% in the past month, highlighting the extent of the sell-off.

Regulators around the world are taking note of the volatility in cryptocurrency markets and are considering new measures to address the risks posed by these assets. In China, the government has announced a crackdown on cryptocurrency exchanges and initial coin offerings (ICOs), while in the United States, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has been stepping up its enforcement efforts against fraudulent ICOs.

The future of cryptocurrency markets remains uncertain, as investors and regulators grapple with the challenges posed by trade policy uncertainty and the lack of regulatory clarity. However, the

data.title: Trump's Funding Freeze Stymies Biden-Era Energy Rebates for Consumers
data.description:
President Donald Trump's administration has ordered a freeze on funding for energy rebates under the Inflation Reduction Act, a move that could significantly impact consumer adoption of energy-efficient products and services. The Inflation Reduction Act, signed into law by President Biden, provided substantial financial support for clean energy initiatives, including rebates for electric vehicle charging stations and energy-efficient home improvements. By pausing the disbursement of these funds, the Trump administration is effectively removing these incentives, which could discourage consumers from adopting energy-efficient alternatives.



The freeze on funding for energy rebates is likely to have long-term economic and environmental consequences. The Inflation Reduction Act was expected to create jobs and stimulate economic growth in the clean energy sector. According to a study by the Rhodium Group, the act could create up to 1.5 million jobs by 2030. Delaying or reducing investment in these initiatives could lead to a loss of these potential jobs and economic opportunities. Additionally, investing in clean energy can help the U.S. achieve energy independence, reducing its reliance on foreign oil. By pausing funding for clean energy initiatives, the U.S. may become more dependent on fossil fuel imports, which can have national security implications.

Furthermore, the Inflation Reduction Act aimed to address climate change by reducing greenhouse gas emissions. By pausing funding for these initiatives, the U.S. may miss its emission reduction targets, exacerbating the impacts of climate change. According to the U.N. Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, limiting global warming to 1.5°C requires rapid, far-reaching and unprecedented changes in all aspects of society. Delaying clean energy initiatives could make it more difficult and costly to achieve these targets.

In conclusion, the freeze on funding for energy rebates under the Inflation Reduction Act is likely to impact consumer adoption of energy-efficient products and services by reducing the financial incentives available to consumers. This could lead to a decrease in the adoption of energy-efficient alternatives, such as electric vehicles and energy-efficient home improvements, which could have negative implications for energy conservation and climate change mitigation efforts. The long-term economic and environmental consequences of delayed or reduced investment in clean energy initiatives are significant and could include job losses, reduced economic growth, increased dependence on foreign oil, and missed emission reduction targets.

data.title: ON Semiconductor: New Name, Falling Stocks
Gene Therapy Breakthrough: MeiraGTx Shares Soar 1.96%!
SMCI Surges Over 20% Pre-Market as Delisting Fears Ease
data.description:
Super Micro Computer has submitted its audited financial results for Q3 and Q4 of 2024 to comply with Nasdaq listing requirements, alleviating market concerns over a potential delisting.

In pre-market trading, super micro computer surged over 20%. 

However, the company warned that despite submitting financial reports, it still faces many risks and challenges related to its previous delinquency in SEC reporting obligations.

As part of the filing, super micro disclosed that it had identified material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting. These weaknesses included IT system issues, lack of documentation for manual journal entries, and insufficient controls to ensure proper segregation of staff duties. The company stated that it is taking corrective actions, including hiring additional accounting and audit personnel and upgrading its IT systems.

Additionally, in Tuesday's filing, Super Micro stated that a special committee of its Board, which reviewed its financial statements, did not believe that Ernst & Young's resignation was "supported by the facts" examined by the committee.

Notably, Super Micro has built a strong partnership with Nvidia, and at one point, its stock outperformed Nvidia's. With Nvidia set to report earnings after today's U.S. market close, investors are keenly watching whether AI infrastructure demand remains strong— a crucial driver of Super Micro's business.

data.title: "Solana ETF Buzz Intensifies, But DTX Token Surges 800% in Presale"
data.description:
Solana ETF speculation intensifies, but a utility coin surges in presale gains, outpacing SOL.

The cryptocurrency market has seen a growing interest in ETFs beyond Bitcoin and Ethereum, with Solana emerging as a focal point for speculation following Grayscale's regulatory filings. While the debate around a Solana ETF heats up, the SOL price has struggled to maintain momentum, dropping 45% this month ahead of a critical $2.2 billion token unlock. Meanwhile, DTX Exchange's presale has caught traders' attention, with its token price surging 800% to $0.18.

The DTX Exchange presale has become one of the most talked-about events in the crypto world this year. With over 700,000 wallets participating, the presale has raised $15.1 million, reflecting growing confidence in the platform's hybrid model that bridges traditional finance and decentralized trading. The platform offers access to over 120,000 assets, from blue-chip stocks to trending altcoins, and allows users to diversify their portfolios with minimal capital.

DTX Exchange's presale success can be attributed to its early-stage accessibility, enterprise-grade infrastructure, and robust security measures. The platform's fractional multi-asset trading feature resonates with both retail and institutional audiences, making it a standout opportunity in the current market cycle. With tokens set to list at $0.36, double the current presale price, many view DTX as a good crypto to buy for exposure to a platform poised to redefine cross-asset trading.

Speculation around a Solana ETF has intensified following Grayscale's recent filing to convert its Solana Trust into a spot ETF. While regulatory approval remains uncertain, the move has sparked debates about SOL's viability as a mainstream investment vehicle. Proponents argue that a Solana ETF would attract institutional capital, but critics highlight concerns over market manipulation risks and the network's recent transactional volume dip.

The SOL price has shown sensitivity to these developments, fluctuating between optimism over ETF prospects and broader market skepticism. Industry observers note that the Solana ETF conversation coincides with a pivotal moment for the network, as over $2.2 billion worth of SOL tokens will unlock on March 1. While some dismiss the unlock as a minor factor

data.title: MORPHO Surges 10% After Coinbase Listing, Boosting Decentralized Lending
data.description:
MORPHO, a decentralized lending protocol, has seen a significant surge in its token price following its listing on coinbase, the leading U.S. cryptocurrency exchange. This listing reflects the growing acceptance of MORPHO among both institutional and retail investors, bolstered by its robust security measures and independent audits, including a $2.5 million bug bounty.

MORPHO's smart contracts have been deployed on major blockchains, enhancing lending efficiency by directly matching liquidity. This innovative approach to decentralized finance has caught the attention of investors, leading to a nearly 10% surge in the token's price after the Coinbase listing.

The Coinbase listing has also brought MORPHO into the spotlight, with the so-called "Coinbase Effect" potentially boosting its market presence. Historical data suggests that assets listed on Coinbase often experience significant volatility, making this listing particularly noteworthy for investors.

MORPHO's unique approach to decentralized lending sets it apart from other protocols. By optimizing lending pools and achieving improved interest rates through direct liquidity matching, MORPHO offers a more efficient lending solution. Its strong security features, including independently audited smart contracts, ensure a safe environment for users.

Following the positive response to its Coinbase listing, MORPHO's team is already planning future enhancements to the platform. These developments may include expanding the asset offerings and enhancing user governance through the native MORPHO token.

Decentralized governance is a core aspect of MORPHO's framework, with the MORPHO token facilitating meaningful participation from the community. This governance model aims to align the interests of stakeholders and provide a transparent approach to decision-making processes, fostering long-term loyalty from users and promoting sustainable growth.

The significant uptick in MORPHO's price following its Coinbase listing illustrates the potential impact of exchange announcements on digital assets. As MORPHO continues to innovate in the decentralized finance space while maintaining a strong focus on security and community governance, the future looks promising for both the platform and its participants.

data.title: Tether's Legal Battle with Bitfinex Intensifies in UK Court
data.description:
Tether, the company behind the stablecoin USDT, has failed to halt a legal dispute with its former bitcoin mining partner, bitfinex, in a UK court. The ongoing case revolves around allegations of market manipulation and fraud, which Tether has consistently denied.

The dispute began in 2019 when Bitfinex, a cryptocurrency exchange, accused Tether of manipulating the market by inflating the price of bitcoin. Bitfinex claimed that Tether had used its stablecoin to artificially boost the price of bitcoin, which benefited both companies. Tether, however, maintains that its stablecoin is fully backed by USD reserves and that it has not engaged in any market manipulation.

The case has been ongoing for several years, with both parties presenting their arguments in court. Tether has attempted to have the case dismissed, but the UK court has refused to do so. The court has also rejected Tether's request for a summary judgment, which would have ended the case in its favor.

The legal battle has had an impact on the cryptocurrency market, with investors and regulators closely watching the developments. The outcome of the case could have significant implications for the stablecoin industry, as it may set a precedent for how stablecoins are regulated and how they interact with other cryptocurrencies.

The case is expected to continue in the UK court system, with a trial date set for later this year. Both Tether and Bitfinex have stated that they are committed to resolving the dispute through the legal process.

data.title: United Parks & Resorts' Q4 2024: Contradictions in EBITDA Growth, Capital Allocation, and Pricing Strategies
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in United Parks & Resorts' latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Expectations for 2025 EBITDA Growth and Impact of New Epic Universe Theme Park, Capital Allocation Priorities, Attendance Growth Strategy, and Pricing Strategy:



Financial Performance and Weather Impact:
- United Parks & Resorts reported near record attendance and record in-park per capita for Q4 2024, despite severe weather impacts like Hurricanes Debbie, Helen, and Milton.
- The combined impact of these weather events resulted in approximately 167,000 guests in Q4 and 432,000 guests in the fiscal year, adjusting for these impacts would have shown a 2% increase in Q4 attendance.
- The company also delivered near record total revenue per capita for the year.

Capital Allocation Strategy:
- United Parks & Resorts repurchased 9.4 million shares or approximately 15% of total shares outstanding in 2024, underscoring their commitment to returning excess cash to shareholders.
- The company maintains a disciplined capital allocation strategy focusing on core CapEx, expansion and ROI CapEx, debt paydown, and share buybacks.
- They plan to spend approximately $225 million of CapEx in 2025, split between $175 million on core CapEx and $50 million on growth and ROI projects.

Strategic Growth Initiatives:
- United Parks & Resorts has several strategic initiatives underway, including the development of hotels, real estate monetization, international licensing, and IP partnerships, expected to yield significant revenue and margin improvements.
- New attractions and rides are planned across parks, such as an immersive flying experience at SeaWorld Orlando, Jewels of the Sea at SeaWorld San Diego, and The Big Bad Wolf: The Wolf's Revenge at Busch Gardens Williamsburg.
- The company is positioning itself to benefit from increased visitation due to the opening of Universal's Epic Universe Park in Orlando.

Cost Efficiency and Management:
- United Parks & Resorts identified approximately $75 million in cost efficiency and reduction initiatives, with $50 million expected to be realized in 2025.
- The cost management strategy is focused on optimizing labor and supply/demand management, utilities, and other operational expenses.
- The company has a track record of delivering on cost management initiatives, as demonstrated by a low growth rate in expenses used to calculate adjusted EBITDA.

data.title: Alamos Gold: 2.59% Surge on Production Boost
